facts: The Andromeda galaxy is approaching us at 250,000 miles per hour
A collision would affect 1 in 100,000 stars, roughly 200 billion stars
The merged galaxy would be 5 times larger than the state of Texas
idea.payoff: and we still don't know the exact outcome
idea.title: Galaxy Collision 🚀
idea.description: Imagine two galaxies, each containing billions of stars, colliding in a cosmic crash 🌠. The Andromeda galaxy is hurtling towards us at an alarming speed, but what would happen if they actually merged? 🤔 Watch to explore the mind-boggling consequences of such an event and ponder the uncertain fate of our universe 🌌

scenes.visual: Two spiral galaxies (Milky Way and Andromeda-like) slowly approaching each other in a dark, star-filled void.

scenes.text: Andromeda is hurtling towards us at 250,000 mph!
scenes.visual: The two galaxies accelerate towards the viewer, showing their intricate arms and central bulges. A speed indicator graphic briefly flashes.

scenes.text: Only 1 in 100,000 stars affected... roughly 200 BILLION stars!
scenes.visual: A dense field of stars, representing a galaxy. A few individual stars briefly pulse or glow brightly, emphasizing the 'affected' ones while the vast majority remain stable.

scenes.text: The merged galaxy would be 5 times larger than Texas!
scenes.visual: An outline map of Texas briefly appears, then rapidly shrinks and is dwarfed by an immense, newly formed elliptical galaxy, showing its gargantuan scale.

scenes.text: The exact outcome remains a cosmic mystery!
scenes.visual: A mesmerizing, chaotic but beautiful visual of a merged galaxy, swirling with nebulae, new star formation, and gravitational arcs, emphasizing the unknown future.
